description:
You are configuring GPredict for an international CubeSat research consortium. Create three ground stations and three modules, ensuring each module is assigned to the correct ground station.

Ground Stations:
1. Delft_TU: 52.0116 N, 4.3571 E, 5m altitude
2. Tokyo_Tech: 35.6042 N, 139.6837 E, 26m altitude
3. CalPoly_SLO: 35.3050 N, 120.6625 W, 96m altitude

Modules:
1. EuropeSats: Include satellites FUNCUBE-1 (AO-73, NORAD 39444) and DELFI-C3 (DO-64, NORAD 32789). Assign to Delft_TU.
2. JapanSats: Include satellites CUTE-1 (CO-55, NORAD 27844), XI-IV (CO-57, NORAD 27848), and SEEDS II (CO-66, NORAD 32791). Assign to Tokyo_Tech.
3. AmericaSats: Include satellites AO-91 (Fox-1B, NORAD 43017) and AO-92 (Fox-1D, NORAD 43137). Assign to CalPoly_SLO.

Do not delete the existing Amateur module. Login with username 'ga' and password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: I'm coordinating an international CubeSat project and need our GPredict set up for three partner universities. First, make three ground stations: Delft_TU in the Netherlands (52.0116 N, 4.3571 E, 5m), Tokyo_Tech in Japan (35.6042 N, 139.6837 E, 26m), and CalPoly_SLO in the US (35.3050 N, 120.6625 W, 96m). Then, create three tracking modules and tie each to its specific ground station so predictions are accurate for that location. Make an 'EuropeSats' module tracking FUNCUBE-1 (39444) and DELFI-C3 (32789) and assign it to the Delft_TU station. Make a 'JapanSats' module tracking CUTE-1 (27844), XI-IV (27848), and SEEDS II (32791) and assign it to the Tokyo_Tech station. Finally, make an 'AmericaSats' module tracking AO-91 (43017) and AO-92 (43137) and assign it to the CalPoly_SLO station. Leave the Amateur module alone.
